---
title: "CV/Resume"
---
# Professional Summary

I am a terrestrial biologist with over five years of experience in the resource conservation sector, working across invasive plant management, terrestrial and aquatic restoration, and long-term small mammal monitoring programs. I hold a master’s degree and have extensive experience working with diverse spatial datasets, with strong proficiency in Python and R for data analysis and ecological modelling.

Since 2020, I have led native seed collection and high-elevation restoration initiatives, implemented integrated pest management strategies, and supported multi-taxa ecological integrity monitoring programs. I thrive in both field-based and office environments and enjoy collaborative work environments where constructive feedback and continuous learning are valued.

---

# Work and Volunteer Experience

## Graduate Teaching Assistant (GTA II)
University of British Columbia  
Jan 2026 – Apr 2026

- Led laboratory sessions for geomatics courses
- Graded laboratory assignments and provided feedback
- Hosted office hours and supported an inclusive learning environment

## Resource Management Technician II
Mount Revelstoke and Glacier National Parks  
May 2022 – Aug 2025

- Planned and led implementation of the invasive plant management strategy for four field seasons
- Assisted in Whitebark pine monitoring including:
  - Seedling assessments
  - Regional health transects
  - Cone caging and tree climbing
  - Seedling planting
  - Habitat suitability analysis
- Supported ecological integrity monitoring programs including:
  - Pika monitoring
  - Amphibian monitoring
  - Bat acoustic monitoring
- Completed remote camera classification and contributed to annual reporting

## Fire and Vegetation Student Technician / Crew Lead
Banff National Park  
Jun 2020 – Nov 2021

- Collected, organized, and visualized geospatial data for long-term vegetation and invasive species management
- Conducted riverine habitat assessments and whirling disease monitoring
- Deployed and maintained water temperature loggers
- Conducted CABIN monitoring using:
  - HACH velocity meters
  - YSI multi-parameter sondes
- Completed the second year of eDNA trials in Johnson Lake

## Student Intern
Land Trust of British Columbia  
Jan 2020 – Feb 2020

- Conducted repeat photography analysis
- Assisted with grant applications
- Developed Passport to Nature educational programming

## UVic Ecological Restoration Club (ERC) — Co-founder
May 2016 – Jun 2019

- Co-founded the club and developed a five-year restoration plan for a degraded Camas meadow on the University of Victoria campus
- Led programs to:
  - Seed native plants
  - Propagate native species
  - Transplant plants for meadow restoration
- Collaborated with restoration organizations including:
  - Galiano Island Conservancy
  - Saanich Native Plants
  - PEPÁḴEṈ HÁUTW̱
  - Native Student Union
  - Satinflower Nurseries

## Outdoor Education Instructor / Guide / Team Leader
YMCA-YWCA of Vancouver Island  
May 2016 – Jun 2019

- Supervised and supported staff members and volunteers
- Planned and guided multi-day kayak, canoe, and hiking expeditions in remote locations

---

# Education

## University of British Columbia
Master of Geomatics for Environmental Management (MGEM)  
Apr 2026

## University of Victoria
B.Sc. Biology with Distinction  
Specialization: Forest Biology and Environmental Studies  
Apr 2021

---

# Publications

- Modelling critical habitat for the Eastern Whip-poor-will (Antrostomus vociferus) in Georgian Bay, Ontario: Comparing the maximum entropy model and multi-criteria decision analysis framework  
- DOI: TBD

---

# Qualifications and Skills

- Experienced in terrestrial, aquatic, and subalpine restoration planning, implementation, and monitoring
- Experienced planning and leading integrated pest management strategies across multiple jurisdictions
- Proficient in ecological modelling using:
  - MaxEnt
  - Multi-criteria decision analysis
- Strong geospatial workflow experience using:
  - ESRI products
  - PostGIS
  - RStudio
  - Python
- Experienced in data cleaning, preprocessing, automation, analysis, and visualization using Python and R
- Extensive field monitoring experience including:
  - Wildlife occupancy (birds, bats, amphibians, mammals)
  - Remote camera classification
  - CABIN monitoring
  - eDNA sampling
  - Terrestrial and aquatic habitat assessments
- Experienced in project design and implementation including multi-year restoration planning and pilot project development
- Over five years of leadership and supervisory experience

---

# Certifications and Workshops

- Wilderness First Aid (50-hour) and Standard First Aid
- FME Workbench Academy I
- Ecological Monitoring Workshop – Integrated Ecological Restoration
- Assistant Pesticide Applicator License
- Swiftwater Rescue I
- Marine Radio Operator’s License
- Pleasure Craft Operator Card
- Assistant Overnight Kayak Guide (AOG)

---

# Professional Associations

- College of Applied Biology — Biologist in Training (Apr 2025 – Present)

---

# Academic Awards

- President’s Scholarship
- Melburn Brenton Scholarship
- Financial bursaries from the University of Victoria based on GPA (2018–2020)
- UVic Dean’s List — Awarded to the top 10% of science students (2016–2020)

---

# Recreational Pursuits

- Rare bird and plant road trips
- Bird watching
- Making pottery
- Hiking
- Kayaking / canoeing
- Cross-country skiing
